reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict stems from a false positive in test code; the flagged clipboard test script isn't executed during build/install and poses no runtime risk.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model incorrectly flagged test script copysecret.py as malicious, while deepseek model didn't review it but correctly assessed other files.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing source tarball (binary file)
qwen failed to verify if flagged test script is executed during build/install
Models prioritized different test files (deepseek reviewed 10, qwen reviewed 10, with minimal overlap)
false_positives: copysecret.py: Test script simulating clipboard operations (qwen's 'unsafe' verdict); normal test pattern for clipboard functionality
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Focus on PKGBUILD hooks and referenced scripts first before test files
Enhance prompt: Explicitly instruct models to verify execution context before flagging test scripts
Add validation: Cross-check file paths against PKGBUILD functions to identify actually executed scripts
Common failure mode: Models overreacting to simulated behaviors in test suites
